page: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The processor configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6a.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark CPU physics and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached read-only bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall within the top 25%. Single-core performance is strong, though single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression score in the middle 50%. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations, achieve top-tier throughput, and static web serving throughput is strong.

The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. It does not include local NVMe SSD storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio and high multi-core processing capacity, the server is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, enterprise database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 128 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional Redis SET throughput, alongside strong software compilation and static web serving capabilities. Single-core performance and text generation speeds are moderate. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is highly suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database operations, and web serving workloads requiring substantial compute and memory resources without local storage dependencies.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 50 Gbps network baseline, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 memory, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, balanced memory allocation, and high-speed baseline networking for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores using AMD EPYC 7R13 processors
Equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory at a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-speed Redis database throughput
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage
Optimized for enterprise databases, multi-threaded applications, and web serving
